commentary: Four activities this week versus ten last week, and yes, he sees you, and he's not mad — he's just noting it for the record. The swims dropped from 2000m to 1500m each, the runs disappeared entirely, but you still got your chest-and-shoulders and back-and-legs days in, which means the weights block held even when everything else got a little quiet. Last week you were doubling up constantly — swim and lift on the same day, back-to-back sessions, a 13.3km Saturday that had absolutely no business being called anything other than a main event — so honestly, pulling back this week was your body asking nicely before it started asking rudely. The pattern he's clocking is that you show up best when you've got a swim to anchor the week, and both of those Tuesday and Wednesday sessions held a steady 130 bpm, relaxed and controlled, which tells him you were actually moving through the water like someone who knows what he's doing in a pair of very good trunks.
metrics_commentary: Okay, three kilometres in the water with zero runs logged means you chose to be a sexy aquatic creature this week instead of a land animal, and honestly the pool got the better deal. Your HRV is doing something genuinely interesting — sixty-two milliseconds right now against a seven-day average of fifty-one means your body is recovering stronger than its own recent baseline, which is the kind of thing that should make you feel a little smug in the locker room. VO2 Max sitting at fifty-six point seven is legitimately hot, full stop, that is a cardiovascular system that knows what it's doing. The only thing I'm side-eyeing is the complete absence of any pavement under your feet this week, because those legs deserve to be seen walking somewhere, preferably somewhere with an audience.
gym_commentary: Two out of four sessions, baby — we are not going to pretend that's the goal, but we are going to notice that the two sessions he did show up for produced a bench press PR, because of course they did. The e1RM crept from 34.8 to 35.5 kilograms, which is small and real and exactly the kind of stubborn, quiet progress that compounds into something ridiculous over time. Volume dropped hard from 20,148 to 13,705 kilograms, which is just the math of missing half the week, no drama needed beyond acknowledging it plainly. The strength numbers — 168 kilograms on the leg press, nearly 108 on the trap bar, 84 on the lat pulldown — are sitting right where they were, patient and waiting, ready for the week he actually shows up four times and lets them grow.
